name: Activated Charcoal
description: A highly porous form of carbon with enormous surface area (500–1,500 m²/g) that adsorbs toxins, impurities, and odors through van der Waals forces — complementing bentonite clay's ionic exchange with non-specific physical adsorption.
appearance: Fine black powder (food/cosmetic grade); granules (filtration grade)
solubility: Insoluble in water and all common solvents
chemistry: Van der Waals adsorption (surface phenomenon). 1g = 1,000+ m² surface area. Micropores (<2nm) for small molecules, mesopores (2–50nm) for medium molecules. Non-selective — binds most organic molecules regardless of charge.
warnings: Inhalation hazard — use dust mask with powder. Non-selective adsorption affects medications (separate by 2+ hours). Stains surfaces black. Not a detox supplement. Does NOT make water microbiologically safe.
shelfLife: Indefinite when kept sealed and dry
